title: UW-Madison Google Workspace - Preserve organizational data
body: This document only covers transferring data that is intended to remain within the UW-Madison Google Workspace.  For recommendations on exporting/backing up personal data, please see UW-Madison Google Workspace - Preserve your data Topics Google Drive Google Calendar Google Drive Files stored in Google Drive include Google Docs, Google Sheets, Google Slides, Google Sites, etc.  When your UW-Madison Google Workspace account is deleted, all files that you own will also be deleted and collaborators will lose access.  Ensure that all files that need to be retained by your department are transferred to another owner prior to your account being deactivated. Find files you own Log in to UW-Madison Google Workspace: UW-Madison Google Workspace - Log in Open Google Drive Search for owner:me or owner: firstname lastname (e.g., owner: bucky badger) Transfer Ownership of Files Transfer Ownership of Files to Another UW-Madison Google Workspace Account Google Drive - Transfer Ownership of Files Note: You can only transfer ownership of files to another UW-Madison Google Workspace account.  You will not be able to transfer ownership of files outside the UW-Madison domain.  This is a limitation by Google and cannot be overridden by the UW-Madison Google Workspace team. Move Files to Shared Drive UW-Madison Google Workspace - Preserve My Drive Data Using a Shared drive (Current UW-Madison affiliates only) Google Calendar When your UW-Madison Google Workspace account is deleted, Google Calendars owned by you will also be deleted.  See this document for instructions on how to transfer Google calendars or events: Google Calendar - Transfer Google calendars or events See Also UW-Madison Google Workspace - Preserve your data UW-Madison Google Workspace - Preserve My Drive Data Using a Shared drive (Current UW-Madison affiliates only)
